search.teamSize: Individual contributor role working closely with site-level operational staff, procurement, and commercial teams across global sites
search.remit: Global trade compliance oversight across US, Mexico, UK, Germany, China, and India manufacturing sites, with primary focus on US export controls and secondary oversight of UK/Germany regulatory requirements
search.coreMission: Ensure global export and import compliance across all Doncasters sites while building out UK/Germany programs to match the established US framework, preventing fines, debarment, and reputational risk in an intensified regulatory environment
search.whyOpen: Replacement — incumbent accepted similar role at another company

compensation.targetBonus: Up to 20% of base salary (discretionary based on multiple factors)

keyCriteria: Ability to Work Cross-Functionally and Influence Across a Global Business
Experience with US Laws and Regulations
Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment
search.confidentiality: Not confidential — SmartSearch can share company name with interested candidates

candidates.name: Katie Connacher
candidates.primaryIndustry: Aerospace and industrial manufacturing, serving power generation, paper mills, wood mills, and military/defense applications
candidates.yearsInRole: Company tenure: 12+ years at Moog Inc. (entire professional career in Industrial Group). Current role tenure: 1 year 2 months (since February 2025)
candidates.totalTeamSize: Individual contributor role. Position designed to have one direct report (specialist level), but requisition not yet approved due to lean business operations. Has trade compliance champions at 30+ international industrial sites who report functionally but not directly
candidates.candidateOverview: Katie Connacher is Trade Compliance Manager at Moog Inc., based in Buffalo, New York, where she has spent her entire 12+ year professional career within the Industrial Group. In her current role since February 2025 (1 year 2 months), she reports to the Director of Global Trade Compliance (legal reporting line) and serves as the primary US regulatory contact for approximately 500 domestic employees plus 30+ international industrial subsidiaries. While structured as an individual contributor due to lean operations, her scope extends across Moog's Industrial Group and she regularly advises other operating entities including Space & Defense, Aircraft Group, and corporate functions. Her remit covers 85-90% industrial applications (power generation, paper mills, wood mills) and 10-15% military/defense work requiring ITAR licensing, with geographic responsibility spanning US domestic sites and international locations where she oversees US export control compliance.
candidates.keyStrength: Deep hands-on expertise in US export controls (ITAR, EAR, OFAC) with proven ability to build compliance programs, influence without authority across 30+ international sites, and operate effectively as individual contributor in lean business environment
candidates.keyConcern: Limited aerospace and defense industry depth—current role is 85-90% industrial applications with only 10-15% military/defense work, potentially creating gap in aerospace-specific classification knowledge and ITAR intensity required for Doncasters' primarily aerospace manufacturing focus
candidates.ourTake: Katie brings solid hands-on US export compliance expertise and has successfully built international compliance programs in a lean environment, which maps well to the Doncasters structure and UK/Germany build-out objective. However, her 85-90% industrial vs. 10-15% defense experience mix is inverted from what Doncasters needs in a primarily aerospace role, and we should validate whether her ITAR classification depth extends beyond the lower-complexity industrial applications that appear to dominate her current portfolio.
candidates.execflowAssessment: Katie Connacher presents as a technically proficient trade compliance professional with deep hands-on US export control expertise (ITAR, EAR, OFAC) and proven ability to operate effectively in lean, entrepreneurial environments as an individual contributor. Her 12+ years at Moog Inc. demonstrate strong cross-functional influence across 30+ international sites without direct authority, C-suite advisory capability, and program build-out experience that directly mirrors Doncasters' UK/Germany compliance framework development objective. However, her experience portfolio is weighted 85-90% toward industrial applications with only 10-15% military/defense work, creating a potential gap in aerospace-specific classification depth and ITAR intensity that would be central to Doncasters' primarily aerospace manufacturing focus. She is not actively seeking a move and values current culture highly, requiring compelling compensation ($150K base preference) and meaningful developmental opportunity to justify transition. Overall, Katie represents a capable hands-on compliance professional whose US regulatory expertise and global program-building skills are strong fits, but whose aerospace industry depth and classification intensity require validation to ensure she can handle the sector-specific demands of this role.

candidates.name: Mary Lund
candidates.primaryIndustry: Aerospace and defense contractor specializing in electronics and infrared technologies
candidates.totalTeamSize: Individual contributor with no direct reports
candidates.candidateOverview: Mary Lund is a Principal Analyst Global Trade at Raytheon, based in the Dallas area, where she has worked remotely for approaching 9 years. She operates as an individual contributor reporting to a Senior Manager of Global Trade within the legal function. Mary manages 12 programs across multiple US locations including McKinney (primary responsibility), Western Massachusetts (backup support), and previously El Segundo, California. Her remit encompasses comprehensive ITAR and EAR compliance management with global cross-functional collaboration responsibilities, working with senior leadership across regions to provide training and influence compliance decisions. She manages both EAR and ITAR regulated products, with the majority being ITAR-controlled defense contractor work focused on electronics and infrared technologies.
candidates.keyStrength: Deep 15-year ITAR/EAR technical expertise with hands-on operational execution managing multiple concurrent compliance programs as remote individual contributor
candidates.keyConcern: Entire career in large defense contractors with no demonstrated mid-sized manufacturing, international regulatory, or program rebuild experience required for core role mandate
candidates.ourTake: Strong technical fit with deep ITAR/EAR expertise, though entire career has been within large defense contractors rather than mid-sized manufacturing environments.
candidates.execflowAssessment: Mary Lund presents as a technically strong candidate with 15 years of deep ITAR/EAR compliance expertise across major aerospace and defense contractors including Raytheon (9 years), L3 Technologies, Elbit Systems, and DRS Technology. Her hands-on experience managing licensing, classification, and export compliance programs—including 12 concurrent programs currently at Raytheon and 25 programs at L3—demonstrates the operational execution capabilities Doncasters requires. She is comfortable as a remote individual contributor, has worked under heightened government scrutiny at both Raytheon and Elbit (FOCI environment), and explicitly enjoys hands-on work. However, her entire career has been within large defense contractors rather than mid-sized manufacturing, and she did not demonstrate explicit experience with international regulatory frameworks (UK/Germany), M&A compliance diligence, or program rebuild initiatives that are central to the Doncasters role. Her motivation to leave stems from recent organizational instability and unclear role definition at Raytheon, seeking an environment with clearer mandate and greater individual impact.

candidates.name: Lauren Sakahara
candidates.yearsInRole: 14 years 3 months at Boeing; 6 years 10 months in current Trade Control Specialist role (since June 2019)
candidates.candidateOverview: Lauren Sakahara is a Trade Control Specialist - Export at Boeing, based in the Seattle area, where she has spent 14 years 3 months with 6 years 10 months in her current role since June 2019. She operates as an individual contributor reporting into Boeing's legal and compliance organization under the corporate division. Lauren primarily supports the 737 commercial airplane program with particular focus on Boeing's Zhoushan China delivery center and the fabrication division, which maintains major international operations across Canada, the UK, and Australia. Her remit encompasses global trade compliance across these manufacturing sites, working as part of an external organization that supports stakeholders throughout the company.
candidates.keyStrength: Deep BIS licensing expertise with proven ability to manage complex raw materials exports and translate technical compliance requirements for diverse stakeholders across global manufacturing operations
candidates.keyConcern: Limited ITAR/DDTC licensing experience and unproven ability to operate in entrepreneurial mid-sized manufacturer environment after 14+ years within Boeing's corporate infrastructure
candidates.ourTake: We believe Lauren presents as an articulate and well-spoken professional with solid BIS licensing expertise, though her ITAR experience is admittedly lighter—she has completed a DFT5 license but acknowledges this is not her primary strength compared to colleagues dedicated to military programs.
candidates.execflowAssessment: Lauren Sakahara brings 14+ years of aerospace industry experience at Boeing with 6+ years specializing in export compliance, demonstrating strong BIS licensing expertise particularly for raw materials exports and proven ability to work with global stakeholders across China, Canada, UK, and Australia. Her M&A due diligence experience across 4-5 Boeing acquisitions and her work under State Department consent agreements align well with Doncasters' active acquisition agenda and compliance program development needs. However, she presents a notable gap in ITAR/DDTC licensing depth—openly acknowledging this is not her primary strength with only one DFT5 license completed—which is concerning given the role's emphasis on deep DDTC proficiency for aerospace parts classification. Her entire career within Boeing's large corporate infrastructure also raises questions about adaptability to Doncasters' more entrepreneurial, mid-sized manufacturing environment where she would lack established support systems and face more resource constraints. While her cross-functional communication skills and technical translation abilities are strong assets, the ITAR experience gap and unproven entrepreneurial agility represent meaningful risks that would require mitigation and close evaluation in subsequent interviews.

compensation.expected: $150,000 - $175,000 total compensation (base + variable bonus); candidate subsequently indicated flexibility to accept $140,000 - $150,000 base + 10% variable bonus

candidates.name: Veaseleti Lupeheke
candidates.primaryIndustry: Aerospace and defense manufacturing, specializing in military defense contracts (target drones, cruise missiles) and civil/commercial aircraft parts
candidates.yearsInRole: 2 years 7 months at Safran (September 2023 to present)
candidates.totalTeamSize: 3 direct reports (1 in Grand Prairie, 2 in San Diego)
candidates.candidateOverview: Veaseleti Lupeheke is currently Trade Compliance Manager at Safran Power Units, based in Grand Prairie, Texas, a role held since September 2023. She oversees trade compliance across two sites—San Diego (civil/commercial aircraft and private jet parts) and Grand Prairie (military/defense contracts including target drones and cruise missiles)—managing a team of three direct reports. She reports to the CEO/VP of the subsidiary and participates in bi-weekly compliance reporting to global headquarters in Paris. Her remit encompasses comprehensive export/import compliance, DDTC licensing, and cross-functional engagement from site operations through executive leadership, with weekly meetings involving the CEO, COO, VPs, and executive team.
candidates.keyStrength: Deep US aerospace/defense export compliance expertise (ITAR, EAR, DDTC) with proven ability to operate hands-on across all organizational levels from site operations to C-suite, demonstrated through 15+ years of progressive responsibility and successful crisis leadership during Safran's tariff response.
candidates.keyConcern: Lack of demonstrated UK/Germany regulatory experience or familiarity despite the role's explicit requirement to assess and potentially rebuild compliance programs in these jurisdictions, which could significantly impact her ability to deliver on a core responsibility without substantial support and ramp-up time.
candidates.ourTake: We believe this candidate presents a compelling profile with deep aerospace/defense compliance expertise and demonstrated ability to operate independently across geographically dispersed operations. Her enthusiasm for the entrepreneurial environment and comfort starting as a sole compliance function owner aligns well with Doncasters' hands-on culture, though we should validate her UK/Germany regulatory familiarity and CMMC/FAR-DFAR experience in follow-up discussions.
candidates.execflowAssessment: Veaseleti Lupeheke presents as a strong technical fit for the Doncasters Global Trade Compliance Manager role, bringing 15+ years of aerospace and defense compliance expertise with demonstrated proficiency in ITAR, EAR, and DDTC licensing across both civil aerospace and military/defense applications. Her current role at Safran mirrors many aspects of the Doncasters opportunity—managing multi-site compliance, engaging weekly with executive leadership, and operating in a hands-on capacity across all departments from supply chain to customs. She has proven crisis management capability through her leadership of Safran's tariff response and demonstrates genuine enthusiasm for Doncasters' entrepreneurial, fast-moving environment, explicitly welcoming the opportunity to start as the sole compliance function owner. However, key gaps require validation: her UK/Germany regulatory experience appears limited to reporting rather than operational oversight, CMMC/FAR-DFAR cybersecurity competencies were not discussed, and M&A diligence experience was not evidenced. Her compensation expectations ($120K-140K) align perfectly with the role's $125K-140K base salary range, and she is open to 15% travel for the fully remote position.

candidates.name: Niklas G
candidates.location: St. Louis metro area (residence in Illinois, office in St. Louis)
candidates.primaryIndustry: Defense manufacturing - US subsidiary of Italian defense contractor Leonardo
candidates.yearsInRole: Company tenure: December 2024 to present (approximately 1 year 4 months per manual notes); role tenure not separately distinguished
candidates.totalTeamSize: Individual contributor role with no direct reports
candidates.candidateOverview: Niklas G is a Senior Export Manager at Leonardo DRS, the US subsidiary of Italian defense contractor Leonardo, based in the St. Louis metro area with residence in Illinois. He has served in this individual contributor role since December 2024, managing all ITAR/EAR licensing, classifications, and export documentation for the land systems division across US operations while beginning cross-training on the naval electronics/power division focused in Southeast Asia. His remit includes coordinating personnel movement globally to support US defense contracts and handling mission equipment packages attached to Striker vehicles, JLTV, and Abrams tanks. He reports to the Senior Director of Trade Compliance, who in turn reports to the VP of Trade Compliance within the legal department structure.
candidates.keyStrength: Deep hands-on ITAR/EAR licensing and classification expertise with proven ability to operate entrepreneurially as an individual contributor in lean, autonomous environments
candidates.keyConcern: Geographic scope expansion from single-site US focus to simultaneous oversight of six-country manufacturing operations may be significant, particularly given limited demonstrated experience building compliance programs under UK/EU regulatory frameworks
candidates.ourTake: We believe Niklas represents a technically strong candidate with the right regulatory expertise and entrepreneurial mindset, though his platform-specific defense background may require some adaptation to the secondary supplier precision manufacturing context at Doncasters.
candidates.execflowAssessment: Niklas G presents as a technically proficient export compliance professional with strong ITAR/EAR licensing expertise gained through defense-focused roles at Leonardo DRS and Boeing, complemented by customs/import experience at Bunge and Eastman Chemical. His current role as Senior Export Manager demonstrates hands-on operational execution managing classifications, licensing, and stakeholder guidance in a lean individual contributor structure with entrepreneurial freedom—directly mirroring Doncasters' requirements. He is explicitly motivated by the opportunity to transition from platform-specific defense work to a secondary supplier role in a smaller, globally-scoped company, and his compensation expectations ($154k target vs. current $140k total package) align well with the role's $125k-$140k base plus 20% bonus structure. The primary consideration is whether his platform-centric defense background (land systems, F-15 aircraft) and current single-site US focus will translate effectively to precision alloy component manufacturing across six countries, particularly given the need to build out UK/Germany compliance programs where his international regulatory exposure appears more limited.

candidates.name: Michael Keller
candidates.primaryIndustry: Aerospace and defense manufacturing with industrial segment
candidates.yearsInRole: 4 years 7 months at Woodward, Inc. (September 2021 – Present)
candidates.totalTeamSize: Manages a team (specific size not disclosed in transcript)
candidates.candidateOverview: Michael Keller is a Sr. Trade Compliance Specialist at Woodward, Inc., a $4B+ publicly traded aerospace and defense manufacturer, based in Bellwood, Illinois. He has been with Woodward for 4 years 7 months, managing trade compliance for the entire aerospace segment across a global footprint spanning the US, Canada, Europe, and Asia. He leads a team responsible for DDTC and Department of Commerce licensing, manufacturing license agreements, technical assistance agreements, foreign visitor programs, technology control plans, and enterprise-wide compliance training across commercial operations, sales, supply chain, shipping, and logistics teams. His remit includes supporting M&A integration with 90-day compliance plans and managing the compliance program for a rapidly growing organization active in aerospace and defense markets.
candidates.keyStrength: Deep US export compliance expertise in aerospace and defense with proven ability to build compliance programs from inception and manage complex global licensing requirements including manufacturing license agreements and technical assistance agreements
candidates.keyConcern: Incomplete interview prevents full assessment of critical factors; transition from team leadership role at larger organization to IC position requires careful exploration to ensure role represents growth opportunity rather than scope reduction
candidates.ourTake: We believe Michael represents an excellent fit for this role, bringing the precise blend of aerospace-focused US export compliance expertise, global stakeholder management, and M&A integration experience that Doncasters requires. His hands-on program-building background and current enterprise-scale responsibility position him well for both the brownfield US refinement and greenfield UK/Germany build components of this mandate.
candidates.execflowAssessment: Michael Keller presents as a highly qualified candidate for the Doncasters Global Trade Compliance Manager role with over 20 years of trade compliance experience and approximately 15 years specifically in aerospace and defense. His current role at Woodward managing the entire aerospace segment compliance program for a $4B+ global manufacturer directly mirrors the Doncasters scope, including DDTC licensing, manufacturing license agreements, technical assistance agreements, foreign visitor programs, and M&A integration with 90-day compliance plans. His deep expertise in US export controls (ITAR, EAR, DDTC) combined with familiarity with UK regulations and proven ability to build compliance programs from inception at NB Coatings and Kellstrom Aerospace positions him well for both refining Doncasters' US operations and building out UK/Germany programs. The primary consideration is his transition from leading a team at a larger organization to an individual contributor role at Doncasters, though the strategic visibility, M&A involvement, and program-building opportunity may offset this. The interview was incomplete, requiring follow-up to address compensation expectations, notice period, team transition concerns, and deeper exploration of his approach to the UK/Germany greenfield build.

A complete follow-up interview is essential before advancing this candidate.

candidates.name: Vivian Imamura-Baker
candidates.primaryIndustry: Aerospace and defense manufacturing, serving military and commercial aviation sectors
candidates.yearsInRole: 1 year 8 months at GE Aerospace (August 2024 to present)
candidates.totalTeamSize: Individual contributor role with no direct reports
candidates.candidateOverview: Vivian Imamura-Baker is Lead Specialist - International Trade Compliance at GE Aerospace in Franklin, Ohio, where she has served for 1 year 8 months. She operates as an individual contributor with no direct reports, serving as the final compliance checkpoint for all US export-controlled shipments across GE's military and commercial aerospace divisions. Her remit covers US export compliance operations including ITAR, EAR, and DDTC licensing verification, classification review, and license tracking across numerous GE manufacturing sites nationwide. Prior to GE, she spent 8.5 years at The Dupps Company where she built the trade compliance program from the ground up, supporting US and Brazil manufacturing operations. She is originally from Brazil and is comfortable with approximately 15% travel.
candidates.keyStrength: Self-directed program builder who thrives in entrepreneurial environments, with proven ability to identify compliance gaps and implement improvements independently across multi-site operations
candidates.keyConcern: Lacks international regulatory experience outside US export controls, which is critical for assessing and rebuilding UK/Germany compliance programs—a core role requirement she explicitly acknowledged as outside her experience
candidates.ourTake: We believe Vivian's entrepreneurial drive and program-building instincts are genuine strengths, but the international regulatory gap she herself identified is a fundamental mismatch for a role requiring UK/Germany program leadership from the outset. Her aerospace depth is too recent to provide the industry credibility Doncasters needs across global stakeholders.
candidates.execflowAssessment: Vivian Imamura-Baker presents as a self-directed compliance professional who thrives in entrepreneurial environments and has built strong US export controls capabilities, but faces critical gaps in international regulatory experience and aerospace industry depth required for this global role. Her 8.5 years building trade compliance programs independently at Dupps Company demonstrates the hands-on, improvement-oriented approach Doncasters seeks, and her current GE Aerospace role provides recent exposure to ITAR/EAR compliance and aerospace context. However, she explicitly acknowledged having no experience with non-US regulations—stating "I do not have experience outside of US" export controls—which directly conflicts with the core requirement to assess and rebuild UK/Germany programs. Additionally, the majority of her compliance expertise was developed in non-aerospace, non-military manufacturing with no licensing requirements, and she lacks hands-on DDTC license preparation experience despite this being identified as a critical technical competency. While her motivation to return to a smaller, high-impact environment aligns well with the role's entrepreneurial nature, and her self-directed learning capability could enable her to develop missing knowledge areas, the combination of international regulatory gap and limited aerospace depth represents significant risk for a role requiring immediate credibility and judgment across global operations. She would require substantial support, external counsel reliance, and development time to build the international regulatory awareness and aerospace-specific expertise the role demands, making her a developmental candidate rather than someone who can step in and lead global program assessment from day one.
